content.text: ## Why
The visual editor's batch-criteria work (story B-UX) must let a user SELECT criteria and prompts
from the library AND CREATE new ones ("full in-editor authoring"). The prompt/criteria library
today supports enumerate (`load_catalog`/`registry.load_criteria`), read (`get_prompt`), and a
committed derived index (`regenerate-index`) — but has NO programmatic WRITE path. In-editor
authoring needs to create/update a prompt-library entry (front-matter + rubric body) and keep the
committed prompt index + the drift CI gate green. This is the data-model layer the editor sits on;
per the epic's sequencing it lands BEFORE the UX.
## What
Add a library WRITE + structured-enumerate data model for prompts and criteria (criteria are
prompt-library entries; D5 — a criterion's id IS its prompt-library id):
- An enumerate API returning the authorable list (id, kind=prompt|criterion, description, inputs/
outputs, is-overlay) for the editor's pickers — a superset of the read-only inspector data.
- A create/update API that writes a prompt-library `.md` (front-matter + body) to the correct home
(`.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` for user prompts; the packaged reviewers dir is read-only at runtime),
validates the front-matter, and regenerates the committed reviewers index so the drift gate stays
green.
- Validation + conflict handling (id collision, malformed front-matter, reserved ids).
## Scope
- New library write/enumerate functions (likely in `rebar.llm.prompts` + a thin criteria view over
`plan_review.registry`), stdlib-only at import.
- Index regeneration wired so a create/update leaves `reviewers/index.json` consistent.
- Unit tests for enumerate shape, create/update round-trip, index regen, and validation/conflict.
- NO editor/frontend code here (that is B-UX); this is the backend data model only.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An enumerate API lists authorable prompts + criteria with the fields the editor pickers need
(id, kind, description, inputs/outputs, is-overlay), covering both packaged and `.rebar/prompts` entries.
- [ ] A create/update API writes a validated prompt-library `.md` to `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` and
regenerates the committed index so the prompt-drift CI gate stays green.
- [ ] Validation rejects id collisions, malformed front-matter, and reserved ids with clear errors.
- [ ] Unit tests cover enumerate, create/update round-trip, index regen, and the validation paths.
- [ ] `make lint` + the full test suite pass.

issuetype.description: Stories track functionality or features expressed as user goals.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.summary: B-DM: Prompt + criteria library write/enumerate/index data model (the editor's backend)

content.text: ## Why
Epic A shipped a basic batch-criteria editor where the user TYPES prompt-ids and overlay
predicates as free text — error-prone and undiscoverable. The plan-review migration (B2) makes the
batch's criteria list the primary thing a human edits, so the editor must let the user SELECT
criteria, prompts, and overlay triggers from lists and CREATE new ones, not hand-type ids/expressions.
## What
Upgrade the workflow visual editor's batch step (and prompt/agent steps) so editing is selection-
and form-driven, for both CRITERIA and PROMPTS:
- **Criterion / prompt fields → dropdowns** sourced from the library enumerate API (B-DM), each
option showing its description (no free-text id typing).
- **Overlay trigger (`when`) → dropdown** of THIS workflow's available triggers (the
`overlay_triggers` step's keyword + structural outputs), rendered as the correct
`${{ steps.<triggers>.outputs.<name> }}` expression, plus an "(always include)" option.
- **Create new overlay trigger** — inline: adds a `{name: [keywords]}` entry to the
`overlay_triggers` step config; it then appears in the dropdown.
- **Create new criterion / prompt — full in-editor authoring**: a form to enter the name + rubric/
body; on save the editor calls the B-DM write API to create the prompt-library `.md` + regen the
index, and references the new id in the step. Must keep the prompt-drift CI gate green.
## Scope
- Editor front-end (`editor_assets/src/`) changes: replace the text fields with library-backed
selects + the authoring form; inject the enumerate data + per-workflow trigger list.
- A small editor-server endpoint to invoke the B-DM create/update API (the editor writes files via
the server, not the browser).
- Real-browser E2E (Playwright) covering: pick-existing criterion/prompt, pick + create overlay
trigger, author a new criterion/prompt end-to-end, and persistence to the IR + library.
- Depends on B-DM (the library write/enumerate data model) and the v3 batch schema (on main).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The batch step's criterion/prompt fields are dropdowns sourced from the library (with
descriptions); the overlay `when` is a dropdown of the workflow's overlay-trigger outputs + "(always)".
- [ ] A user can create a new overlay trigger inline (adds it to the `overlay_triggers` step) and
immediately select it.
- [ ] A user can author a new criterion AND a new prompt in-editor (name + rubric/body); on save it
is written to the library via the B-DM API, the index is regenerated, and the new id is referenced
in the step.
- [ ] A real-browser E2E proves pick-existing, create-trigger, and author-new-criterion/prompt end
to end with persistence to the IR + library; the prompt-drift gate stays green.
- [ ] `make lint` + the full test suite pass.

issuetype.description: Stories track functionality or features expressed as user goals.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.summary: B-UX: Visual editor — select/create criteria, prompts & overlay triggers (pickers + in-editor authoring)

content.text: ## Why

A fake is only as good as its fidelity to the real client. If `FakeAcliClient` (story A) drifts from the real `AcliClient`, we reintroduce bug 0ee6's failure mode — tests pass against fictional shapes. Google's "Software Engineering at Google" prescribes the **verified fake**: the fake carries contract tests written against the API's public interface, and those same tests run against BOTH the real implementation and the fake.
## What

- **Shape-contract tests** for the `AcliClient` enrichment surface (`get_comment_map`, `get_issuelinks_map`, `get_parent_map`, `search_issues`) asserting the RETURN SHAPE (keys present, JSON primitive types) — not exact values.

- A parametrized harness that runs the identical contract tests against (a) the `FakeAcliClient` (always, hermetic) and (b) the real `AcliClient` against live Jira (behind the existing live/external pytest marker, opt-in).
- The fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's, so a future real-API change surfaces as a fake-drift failure.
- Optional: validate the real client's responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI where the spec covers the field (investigate spec completeness first — see epic open question).
## Scope
- In: the dual-run (real + fake) shape-contract harness for the enrichment methods.
- Out: capturing the fixtures (story A); the snapshot round-trip (story B).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shape-contract tests for the enrichment methods run against the fake (always) and the real client (live-gated marker).
- [ ] The fake fails the contract test if its return shape diverges from the real client's return shape.
- [ ] Assertions check structure/type (keys + JSON primitive types), not exact values (anti-change-detector).
- [ ] Running against live is opt-in (does not flake or slow the default suite).

issuetype.description: Stories track functionality or features expressed as user goals.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.summary: Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient

content.text: ## Why

The live e2e probe encoded the inbound-comment bug as EXPECTED behavior (`Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap … NOT-SYNCED`, and would have FAILED if comments synced), so the defect was undetectable; no probe round-trips relationships at all; and the probes are manual, not in CI. A probe that locks a bug in as "expected" is worse than no probe.
## What

- A **hermetic bidirectional round-trip assertion** — using story A's fixtures/fake through the production path — for comments, issue-links (both directions), and parent, runnable in CI without live Jira.

- **De-encode the "expected-bug" guards**: convert probe assertions that lock in a current gap (e.g. `Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap NOT-SYNCED`) into `xfail`/fail-when-the-gap-closes, so a fix is detected rather than rejected. Audit `scripts/jira-pressure-test/*.sh` for every `NOT-SYNCED`/"as expected" guard.
- A documented (optionally scheduled) live round-trip variant for the directions that truly need real Jira, kept off the default suite so it never flakes CI.
## Scope
- In: the hermetic round-trip test; the probe de-encoding audit; the live-variant doc.
- Out: the producer↔consumer schema/contract (story B).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A hermetic test asserts bidirectional round-trip of comments, links, and parent through the production path (no live Jira needed).
- [ ] Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as "expected/NOT-SYNCED" is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes (xfail) or removed; none silently passes on a defect.
- [ ] The hermetic round-trip runs in CI; any live variant is opt-in/scheduled and documented.
- [ ] Assertions check round-trip outcomes, not whole-blob equality (anti-change-detector).

issuetype.description: Stories track functionality or features expressed as user goals.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.summary: Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards

content.text: ## Why

This is the keystone that closes the bug class. The fetcher (producer) and the differs/appliers (consumers) share an implicit contract — the snapshot-entry dict shape — that nothing pins or jointly exercises. Pin it once, and exercise producer→consumer together through the production path, so a key/shape divergence (the exact failure in bugs 0ee6 and 3f04) fails a test immediately.
## What

- A single source-of-truth **snapshot-entry schema** (TypedDict + JSON Schema) in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_snapshot_schema.py`, covering every field the differs read: `summary`, `description` (ADF), `status`, `priority`, `assignee`, `labels`, `comment` (nested), `issuelinks`, `parent`. Documented as the canonical shape both sides target.

- A **producer→consumer round-trip contract test** (`tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_snapshot_contract.py`): the `FakeAcliClient` (story A) → `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` → `inbound_differ` + `outbound_differ` → applier, through the PRODUCTION path. It asserts each consumer-depended key/shape is actually produced and read correctly (comment, issuelinks, parent + scalars). Per the epic sequencing, land it FIRST on the `comment`/`issuelinks` seam, then broaden.

- Validation asserts **structure/type, not exact values** (Dredd/Gavel posture): `fetch_snapshot` output conforms to the schema; the differs emit the expected mutations for the captured payloads. No whole-blob golden equality; no mock-call-count.

- **Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap):** commit a documented **anti-change-detector guardrail** for reconciler tests — a short `docs/` note (ADR-style) that records the testing-strategy decision (why one producer↔consumer contract test through the production path, rather than Pact or a live sandbox), where the snapshot schema lives, and the rules new tests must follow (assert semantic round-trip + structure/type; no mock-call-count; no whole-blob golden equality). New tests reference it.
## Scope
- In: the schema module, the joint round-trip contract test, and the documented guardrail/ADR note.
- Out: capturing fixtures (story A), collapsing appliers (story D), telemetry (story E).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_snapshot_schema.py` defines the snapshot-entry shape (TypedDict + JSON Schema) as the single source of truth.
- [ ] A test validates `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` output against the schema, and would FAIL on the pre-fix conditions (issuelinks absent; comment under the wrong key).

- [ ] A round-trip test runs fetcher → differs → applier through the production path and asserts comment/issuelinks/parent plus scalar fields read correctly; it fails if a producer key/shape diverges from a consumer read.
- [ ] All assertions are semantic (keys/types/round-trip), never whole-blob golden equality or interaction/mock-call-count.
- [ ] A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note recording the testing-strategy decision + the schema location + the rules) is committed and referenced by the new tests.

issuetype.description: Stories track functionality or features expressed as user goals.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.summary: Producer<->consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path

content.text: ## Why
The migration deliberately runs the bespoke plan-review gate and the new workflow gate in
parallel, validated by the B4 planned-trace parity harness, and B5 cuts the live gate over to
the workflow while keeping the bespoke path reachable behind a flag as a fallback. Once parity
is EXHAUSTIVELY validated and the workflow gate has run as the sole live gate without regression,
the scaffolding should come down — both the parity harness (a migration-validation tool) and the
bespoke `orchestrator.run_review` path (now dead weight). Convergence: only what earns its keep
remains. Without this story the epic leaves two parallel gate implementations + a harness in the
tree indefinitely.
## What
Retire the migration scaffolding once the workflow gate is proven:
- Remove the B4 **parity harness** (`tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_parity.py`,
`_parity_corpus.py`, and the `TracingFakeRunner` scaffolding in `trace.py` if unused elsewhere).
- Retire the **bespoke plan-review path**: `orchestrator.run_review` + the bespoke-only fan-out
(the `ThreadPoolExecutor` Pass-1 driver) + the B5 fallback flag, so the engine workflow is the
SOLE plan-review gate. Keep the SHARED machinery the workflow still uses (`pass1.run_pass1`,
`det_floor`, `registry`, `passes`, `sizing`, `attest`, `ProductionBatchRunner`).
- Preserve forwarding shims / public API for anything still referenced (`review_plan` library/CLI/
MCP entrypoints keep working, now backed only by the workflow path).
## Scope
- This is the FINAL convergence step; it must come AFTER B5 (cutover) and after parity is
exhaustively validated (the corpus is comprehensive AND a soak period with the workflow as the
sole live gate showed no regression — record the evidence on this ticket before deleting).
- Delete dead bespoke code + the harness; update tests/docs; ensure the close/claim gates keep
working through the workflow path with signatures intact.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parity is recorded as exhaustively validated (comprehensive corpus + a documented soak with
the workflow as the sole live gate, no regression) BEFORE any deletion.
- [ ] The B4 parity harness + its corpus + the unused tracing scaffolding are removed.
- [ ] The bespoke `orchestrator.run_review` path + its `ThreadPoolExecutor` fan-out + the B5
fallback flag are removed; the engine workflow is the sole plan-review gate; `review_plan`
library/CLI/MCP entrypoints still work (forwarding shims preserved).
- [ ] No orphaned references to the removed code; `make lint` + the full test suite pass.

issuetype.description: Stories track functionality or features expressed as user goals.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.summary: B-RETIRE: retire the parity harness + the bespoke plan-review path once parity is exhaustively validated

content.text: ## Context
Three production Jira-sync bugs escaped our tests this cycle, all the same CLASS — a gap at a *seam* that no test or probe spanned:

- **Inbound comments (bug 0ee6):** the inbound differ read snapshot key `comments`, but the fetcher writes the nested Jira-REST key `comment` — producer and consumer were unit-tested in isolation, so their shared shape was never jointly exercised.

- **Issue-links both directions (bug 3f04):** outbound link dispatch existed only in a *typed-leaf* applier that production does not use (production routes through the *batch* applier, which dropped links); inbound links were invisible because the snapshot never carried `issuelinks`.
- **A live e2e probe encoded the inbound-comment bug as EXPECTED** (`Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap … NOT-SYNCED`), so the defect could never be detected.

Root causes: (1) no producer↔consumer contract — differs are unit-tested against hand-built snapshot dicts in shapes the real fetcher never emits; (2) two outbound applier paths drift (tests cover the dead typed leaf; production uses the batch path; capabilities were re-added one bug at a time — parent 8b25, comments 87e4, links 3f04); (3) sub-op success is silent (the batch outcome reports mutation-level `error=None`, never `links_applied`/`comments_applied`); (4) live probes are manual, not in CI, do not round-trip relationships, and can encode bugs as expected.

Deep research (Pact consumer-driven contracts; vcrpy semantic matchers; Schemathesis + Dredd/Gavel structure-not-value validation; Google SWE-book "verified fakes" and its change-detector warning; and how real Jira clients test — `jira-python` uses a live Dockerized/Cloud sandbox, while `jira-cli` serves on-disk fixtures *captured from real Jira* THROUGH its production serialization via an in-process mock) converges on a layered strategy right-sized for a single team: **one producer↔consumer snapshot contract test, fed by hermetic-but-honest fixtures derived from real Jira payloads, exercised through the PRODUCTION code path** — explicitly avoiding change-detector bloat. Full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact are out of scope (overkill for a single team).
## Child stories
This epic decomposes into six stories (sized for one PR each):
- **A — Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client** (`fe57-7712-1e3f-45f4`): the foundation — fixtures captured from real Jira, served through the production path.
- **B — Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test** (`8937-eed3-c881-4ea1`): the keystone; also owns the documented anti-change-detector guardrail artifact.
- **C — Verified fake: contract tests against BOTH real and fake client** (`59ef-ff3e-5aea-47b1`): keeps the fake honest.
- **D — Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one** (`33d0-1e2c-e059-4181`).
- **E — Sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary** (`2359-ac1a-7c76-4f56`): depends on D.
- **F — Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode "expected-bug" guards** (`822a-2bad-fe48-43e0`).
## Sequencing (MVP-first thin vertical slice)
The epic ships incrementally, not as a big-bang:

1. **A → B scoped FIRST to the known seam** — capture the inbound-comment (`comment`) and `issuelinks` shapes (bugs 0ee6/3f04) and land the single round-trip contract test on exactly those, before broadening the schema to all fields. This thin vertical slice de-risks the whole contract approach against the canonical failure.
2. **C** once the fake exists (keep it honest).
3. **D then E** — telemetry builds on the single consolidated applier.
4. **F** reuses A's fixtures for the hermetic round-trip and de-encodes the probe guards.
Each story is independently shippable behind its own PR.
## Rollout & rollback

- The **fail-loud sub-op invariant** (story E) lands **warn-first** (telemetry + `WARNING`) behind a flag/canary, and is promoted to hard-fail only after a baseline pass that emits zero such warnings; revert = drop back to warn (no other code change).
- The **applier collapse** (story D) verifies behavioral parity — the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did — **before** the retired path is deleted.
## Success Criteria
- Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing.
- Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path.
- A silent sub-op no-op (e.g. links computed but never applied) fails a test/canary instead of passing green.
- Bidirectional round-trips (comments, links, parent) for the real Jira shapes are asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected.
- New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic round-trip, structure/type); the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema module exists as the canonical shape.
- [ ] A test validates the fetcher's snapshot output against that schema.
- [ ] A test validates each differ's snapshot reads against that schema.
- [ ] One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads.
- [ ] Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains).
- [ ] The retired outbound applier path is deleted.
- [ ] Outbound sub-op telemetry (`links_applied`/`comments_applied`/`labels_applied`) is surfaced in the structured batch outcome.
- [ ] A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout in Rollout & rollback.
- [ ] Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically through the production path.
- [ ] Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes.
- [ ] A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B).
- [ ] All six child stories are closed; the full reconciler suite is green.

issuetype.description: Workstreams track a set of related work and activities that work towards a larger deliverable.
issuetype.name: Epic

fields.summary: Close the Jira-sync producer<->consumer & tested-vs-shipped test-coverage gap class

content.text: ## Why

The escaped bugs all began with unit tests feeding the differs hand-built `jira_snapshot` dicts in shapes the real fetcher never produces (flat `comments` instead of nested `comment`; `issuelinks` the snapshot never carried). The converged fix (per `jira-cli`'s pattern) is **fixtures captured from real Jira payloads, served through the production serialization path** — never hand-massaged. This is the foundation every other story in the epic builds on.
## What

- Capture **scrubbed real Jira REST payloads** for the exact responses the fetcher consumes: the paged `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql`, plus the enrichment maps `get_comment_map` (`{"comment":{"comments":[…]}}`), `get_issuelinks_map` (`{"issuelinks":[…]}`), and `get_parent_map` (`{"parent":{"key":…}}`). Store under `tests/fixtures/jira/`.

- A `FakeAcliClient` (in `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py`) that returns those fixtures from the real method signatures (`search_issues`, `get_comment_map`, `get_issuelinks_map`, `get_parent_map`) so they flow through the production `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` enrichment — the fake adds NO shape massaging.
- Secret-scrubbing on capture (strip API tokens, emails, accountIds) via layered redaction (vcrpy-style), so committed fixtures carry no secrets.
- A `scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py` (live-gated) + documented re-capture procedure so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors.
## Scope
- In: captured fixtures + the fake client + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc.
- Out: the contract test that consumes them (separate story), the verified-fake honesty check (separate story).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/fixtures/jira/*.json` hold scrubbed real Jira payloads for search + comment + issuelinks + parent (verified: no tokens/emails/accountIds present).
- [ ] `FakeAcliClient` serves the fixtures through the production fetcher enrichment path with no shape transformation.
- [ ] `scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py` captures + scrubs from a live Jira (gated) and the re-capture cadence is documented.
- [ ] Fixtures are consumed only via the production code path (no test reads them and hand-reshapes).

issuetype.description: Stories track functionality or features expressed as user goals.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.summary: Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)
